seoTitle: How to Edit an Invoice in QuickBooks Online Easily
metaDescription: Update invoice details, item quantities, and discounts in QuickBooks Online to keep billing accurate and improve cash flow.
intro: Invoices don't always get everything right on the first try, and QuickBooks Online makes it straightforward to fix that. This guide covers updating invoice details, adjusting quantities, and applying discounts, which matters most for freelancers and small businesses that send multiple invoices where billing accuracy directly affects cash flow.

steps.heading: Access the invoice you need to edit
steps.body: Find the invoice in QuickBooks Online and open it to view its current details.

steps.heading: Update invoice details
steps.body: Adjust customer information, line items, or dates as needed to reflect the correct billing details.

steps.heading: Adjust item quantities and pricing
steps.body: Change quantities, rates, or add discounts so the invoice total accurately reflects what's owed.

steps.heading: Save the updated invoice
steps.body: Confirm your edits and save the invoice so the changes are reflected in your records and any linked reports.

steps.heading: Resend if the customer already received it
steps.body: If the invoice was already sent, resend the corrected version so the customer has the accurate copy.

faq.question: Can I edit an invoice after it's been sent to a customer?
faq.answer: Yes, you can still edit it in QuickBooks Online, but you should resend the updated version so the customer has accurate information.

faq.question: What happens if I edit an invoice that's already been paid?
faq.answer: Editing a paid invoice can create mismatches between the payment and the new total, so it's best to review payments before making changes.

faq.question: Can I add a discount to an existing invoice?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online lets you add discounts directly to invoice line items or the total when editing.

faq.question: Does editing an invoice affect my inventory records?
faq.answer: If the invoice includes tracked inventory items, changing quantities can affect your inventory counts, so it's worth double-checking those numbers.
